mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when both proxies decline simultaneously compared to periods where only one declines.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances indicate resilient household spending that offsets tighter financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when credit expands under restrictive rates compared to baseline, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples stabilize.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0 percent signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative forward returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises above the threshold, or the signal's predictive power decays below random chance over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with stable federal funds rates indicates a soft-landing scenario where wage growth supports corporate revenues without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not outperform when employment rises and rates stabilize, or the interaction effect reverses sign during historical soft-landing periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, accelerating consumer credit balances indicate desperate household borrowing that precedes default waves, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as financial stress materializes.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when credit accelerates under high unemployment, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in historical credit boom bust cycles.
